name: Chocolate Chunk Brownies
recipeIngredient: 9 tablespoons Unsalted butter, cut into 1/4 cup
1 1/2 cups Coarsely chopped walnut halves, divided
14 ounces Swiss dark chocolate, cut into 1/4-inch chunks, divided
recipeInstructions:
1. Allow the brownies to set at room temperature for at least 6 hours or overnight before cutting.
2. Preheat oven to 325°F. Line a 13-by-9 1/2-inch baking pan with double thickness of aluminum foil extending 2 inches beyond short ends. Fold overhang and butter bottom of foil-lined pan.
3. In a medium bowl, stir together flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
4. Place semisweet chocolate in a large bowl.
5. In a medium saucepan, combine sugar, butter, corn syrup, and water. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly with wooden spoon until butter melts, sugar dissolves, and mixture boils. Remove from heat and pour hot syrup over chocolate. Let stand 1-2 minutes to melt chocolate. Whisk smooth.
6. Whisk in eggs one at a time, blending until smooth. Whisk in vanilla and flour mixture, mixing until batter is smooth. Fold in 1 cup walnuts and 6 ounces Swiss chocolate chunks.
7. Scrape batter into prepared pan and spread evenly with spatula. Sprinkle remaining 1/2 cup walnuts and 3 ounces chocolate chunks on top. Bake for 40-50 minutes or until a cake tester inserted into center comes out with few moist crumbs clinging to it.
8. Cool brownies in pan set on wire rack. When completely cool, cover pan with plastic wrap and let set at room temperature for at least 6 hours or overnight.
9. Invert brownies onto large plate or cutting board. Remove pan and carefully peel off foil. Invert again onto smooth cutting surface and cut into 24 bars. Store at room temperature in airtight container for up to five days.
